zotero.abstractNote: Deep Metric Learning (DML) aims to learn an embedding space where similar data points are close and dissimilar ones are far apart. While proxy-based methods have recently achieved state-of-the-art performance, they often suffer from the "collapsing" of embeddings, where the learned representations occupy only a small subspace of the available hypersphere. This phenomenon, often referred to as the "isotropy problem", limits the discriminative power of the learned embeddings. In this paper, we propose a novel regularization technique, Non-isotropy Regularization (NIR), that explicitly encourages the learned embeddings to be non-isotropic, thereby preventing collapse and improving the quality of the learned representations. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several standard DML benchmarks, where it consistently outperforms existing proxy-based methods.
zotero.conferenceName: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
